generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Resistive heating element with adjustable power output for thermal incubation, operating within defined temperature range. Limited by power supply and thermal mass, may exhibit hysteresis during rapid adjustments. Calibrated via Digital Twin simulations to ensure uniform temperature profiles within specified tolerances."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Forced-air distribution system with variable speed or on/off operation to homogenize heat within the Physical Twin. Specifications include sufficient flow rate for uniform temperature distribution, constrained by motor specifications (RPM limits, noise levels) and aerodynamic losses."
]

instance ControllerHardware : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Dedicated hardware/software interface (microcontroller or PLC) that communicates bidirectionally with the Digital Twin via defined protocol. Implements safety constraints including maximum temperature thresholds, runtime limits, and emergency shutdown protocols for overheating or mechanical stress prevention."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TopTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer positioned at the top layer of the Physical Twin for real-time temperature monitoring. Provides ±0.5°C accuracy with 12-bit resolution (0.0625°C increments). Operates within -10°C to +85°C range."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TopTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance MiddleTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer positioned at the middle layer of the Physical Twin for real-time temperature monitoring. Provides ±0.5°C accuracy with 12-bit resolution (0.0625°C increments). Operates within -10°C to +85°C range."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MiddleTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance BottomTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer positioned at the bottom layer of the Physical Twin for real-time temperature monitoring. Provides ±0.5°C accuracy with 12-bit resolution (0.0625°C increments). Operates within -10°C to +85°C range."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BottomTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TopTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TopTemperatureSensor
]

instance MiddleTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MiddleTemperatureSensor
]

instance BottomTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BottomTemperatureSensor
]

instance CombinedTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TopTemperatureSensor, MiddleTemperatureSensor, BottomTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ThermalBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based differential equation model representing thermal dynamics, heat transfer, and system responses to perturbations within the Physical Twin. Used for reproducing and predicting temperature profiles under current and hypothetical conditions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQMessageBroker, PythonScipyNumericalEngine
]

instance SpatialTemperatureGradientModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model capturing spatial temperature variations across the Physical Twin's three sensor positions, used for validating uniform temperature distribution and identifying thermal gradients."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonScipyNumericalEngine
]

instance TopTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw and processed temperature readings from the top layer sensor, including time-series data with ±0.5°C precision. Used for calibration and state estimation algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TopTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, StateEstimator
]

instance MiddleTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw and processed temperature readings from the middle layer sensor, including time-series data with ±0.5°C precision. Used for calibration and state estimation algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MiddleTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, StateEstimator
]

instance BottomTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw and processed temperature readings from the bottom layer sensor, including time-series data with ±0.5°C precision. Used for calibration and state estimation algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BottomTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, StateEstimator
]

instance CombinedTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated temperature data from all three sensors, used for cross-validation, spatial analysis, and overall system state estimation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CombinedTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, FaultDetector, SupervisoryController
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ server facilitating asynchronous communication between Physical Twin sensors, data processing components, and Digital Twin services. Provides message persistence and distribution to calibration algorithms and monitors."
]

instance PythonScipyNumericalEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python Scipy library for numerical computations including solving differential equations for thermal dynamics models and implementing calibration algorithms to refine model parameters."
    DTDFVocab:enables CalibrationService, MonitoringService, SimulationService
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Statistical or optimization-based algorithm (e.g., least squares, Bayesian updating) that adjusts model parameters (thermal conductivity, heat capacity) using real-time sensor data to maintain model accuracy."
]

instance StateEstimator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filter or particle filter implementation that estimates hidden states (e.g., internal temperature gradients) from noisy sensor measurements, reducing uncertainty in system state reconstruction."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, ControlService
]

instance FaultDetector : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithm analyzing sensor data streams to identify anomalies (sensor drift, actuator failures) by comparing observed Physical Twin behavior against simulated expectations."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, ControlService
]

instance SupervisoryController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Rule-based or model-predictive control algorithm generating corrective actions (e.g., heating setpoint adjustments) based on Digital Twin-generated insights."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous collection of temperature sensor data from the Physical Twin via 1-Wire bus, transmission to RabbitMQ, and initial processing for real-time performance tracking."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureDataInsight, SystemStateInsight
]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of Digital Twin models using real-time sensor data to refine thermal parameters, ensuring models remain aligned with evolving Physical Twin behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, ParameterOptimizationAction
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time state estimation and safety violation detection comparing Physical Twin behavior against simulated expectations, triggering alerts for temperature excursions or anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyViolationAlert, PerformanceMetricInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supervisory control system generating actuator commands (e.g., heatbed/fan adjustments) based on Digital Twin insights to maintain optimal system performance and safety."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuatorAdjustmentAction, EmergencyShutdownAction
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic model simulations reproducing and predicting Physical Twin behavior under current and hypothetical conditions, enabling scenario testing and predictive analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveBehaviorInsight, ScenarioAnalysisInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature readings from all three sensor positions with spatial distribution analysis, used for validating uniform temperature profiles."
]

instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Reconstructed internal state of the Physical Twin including temperature gradients and potential anomalies detected through state estimation algorithms."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Confidence metrics indicating the accuracy of Digital Twin models based on calibration algorithm performance and parameter estimation quality."
]

instance ParameterOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of model parameters to improve predictive accuracy, triggered by calibration service based on real-time sensor discrepancies."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SafetyViolationAlert : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of temperature excursions beyond safe operating thresholds or other safety-critical deviations from expected behavior."
]

instance PerformanceMetricInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of system performance including energy efficiency, temperature stability, and operational health metrics."
]

instance PredictiveBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulated predictions of Physical Twin behavior under current or hypothetical conditions, enabling proactive decision-making."
]

instance ScenarioAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Results from testing hypothetical scenarios (e.g., actuator failures, environmental changes) to evaluate system robustness."
]

instance ActuatorAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generated commands to adjust heatbed power output or fan operation based on Digital Twin insights to maintain optimal temperature profiles."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EmergencyShutdownAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated emergency response triggering gradual ramp-down or immediate shutdown of actuators when safety violations are detected."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance ThermalIncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Biomedical or industrial environment requiring precise thermal control, including both internal Physical Twin conditions and external ambient temperature fluctuations."
    base:contains PhysicalTwinSystem, ExternalEnvironment
]

instance PhysicalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Thermal incubator Cyber-Physical System including sensors, actuators, and control mechanisms for precise environmental temperature regulation."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorEnvironment
]

instance ExternalEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Ambient conditions affecting the Physical Twin including temperature variations, humidity levels, and potential external disturbances."
    base:isContainedIn ThermalIncubatorEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin employs a **bang-bang controller logic** implemented on a **Raspberry Pi** to generate control commands for the Physical Twin’s actuators (e.g., fan and heatbed). Commands are transmitted via the same **RabbitMQ server** used for data acquisition, ensuring synchronized communication. A **safe protocol** is incorporated into the low-level driver to validate and authenticate commands before execution, preventing unsafe operations. Feedback from the PT is not explicitly described, but the system assumes real-time monitoring of actuator responses. Conflict resolution is not detailed, though the use of a centralized controller suggests hierarchical decision-making. Command execution confirmation is implied through the system’s monitoring and calibration feedback loop."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates within a **closed-loop temporal framework** where data collection from the Physical Twin (PT) is performed through periodic sensor readings, though explicit synchronization frequencies between the PT and Digital Twin (DT) components are not detailed in the provided documents. The system emphasizes **real-time calibration** of models via algorithms (e.g., state estimators, fault detectors) to update the cyber-physical system (CPS) and environmental models dynamically. While the document references a **physics-based constraint** (e.g., 'under 2 seconds' for heating capacity), this pertains to the PT's physical response time rather than explicit DT synchronization or model update intervals. No granular temporal specifications (e.g., latency requirements for monitoring or simulation services) are provided beyond the implicit need for low-latency feedback loops to enable safe and optimal operation, as implied by the closed-loop architecture. The temporal granularity is dictated by the PT's operational dynamics and the algorithms' ability to process sensor data in near-real-time for decision-making (e.g., safety checks or optimizations)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system follows a **modular architecture pattern** that integrates three core components: **Data** (collected from the Physical Twin via sensors), **Models** (abstract representations of the PT’s cyber and physical aspects), and **Algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, supervisory controllers, state estimators, and optimizers). The system employs a **closed-loop orchestration mechanism**, where calibrated models inform **monitoring algorithms** that assess system safety and optimality. When violations are detected, the system enables **human-in-the-loop or automated interventions** to adjust the Physical Twin’s parameters or operations. Resource management is implied through the **dynamic updating of models** via calibration algorithms, ensuring computational efficiency and scalability. The DT framework adheres to a **service-oriented design**, where each component (data acquisition, model calibration, monitoring) operates as an interdependent module, coordinated to maintain alignment between the digital and physical domains. No explicit mention of **cloud-edge architectures**, **microservices**, or **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, Apache Airflow) is provided, but the described workflow aligns with a **centralized, algorithm-driven constellation**."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    The document outlines key aspects of the Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle implicitly through its description of the framework's components and integration with the Physical Twin (PT). While explicit lifecycle phases are not detailed, the following can be inferred:

    - **Development Phase**:
      - **Representation Types**: Models are initially abstract representations (e.g., CAD models for geometry, dynamic models for behavior) built to understand specific aspects of the system. These models are later integrated into the DT framework.
      - **Transition Management**: The document emphasizes that models used during development should not be discarded but **integrated into the DT** post-deployment, implying a transition from standalone modeling to a DT framework.
      - **Lifecycle Governance**: The DT framework is described as a **continuous integration of modeling, simulation, monitoring, and optimization**, suggesting iterative refinement and governance through real-time data and algorithmic updates (e.g., calibration algorithms for parameter estimation).

    - **Operational Phase**:
      - The DT operates by **collecting data from the PT via sensors**, updating models (e.g., calibration algorithms), and enabling **predictive simulations** to analyze current/emerging conditions.
      - **Monitoring and Control**: The DT includes **monitors for safety/optimality checks**, with human or algorithmic intervention for corrective actions (e.g., adjusting the PT based on violations detected).

    - **Evolution Phase**:
      - The document implies **dynamic evolution** through continuous data-driven updates (e.g., fault detection, state estimation) and optimization, though explicit versioning or governance frameworks are not mentioned.

    **Not explicitly detailed**: Phases like prototyping, validation, or decommissioning are not addressed.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The **fidelity and validity** of the Digital Twin (DT) are ensured through rigorous **model validation, verification, and uncertainty quantification**. Key aspects include:
- **Model fidelity requirements**: High fidelity is achieved by integrating **geometric, behavioral, and analytical models** that align with the PT’s physical and cyber characteristics. Fidelity is validated via **simulation reproducibility** under current operating conditions.
- **Validation and verification methods**: Models are validated against **real-world PT data** to ensure accuracy in predicting behavior. Verification involves **cross-checking simulations** with empirical observations (e.g., comparing predicted vs. actual system responses).
- **Uncertainty sources and quantification**: Sources of uncertainty include **sensor noise, environmental variability, and model simplifications**. These are quantified via statistical methods or **error propagation analysis** to assess model robustness.
- **Error detection and correction mechanisms**: The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to adjust model parameters dynamically, correcting discrepancies between simulated and observed PT behavior. **Fault detection algorithms** identify anomalies in real-time data, triggering corrective actions.
- **Quality assurance processes**: Models and data undergo **iterative refinement** through feedback loops, ensuring continuous improvement in fidelity. The DT framework enforces **safety and optimality checks** to validate system performance against operational constraints."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s technical network infrastructure consists of a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B** hosting both the controller logic and the **RabbitMQ server**, connected to the Physical Twin via **Wi-Fi**. No explicit mention of network topology, bandwidth requirements, or latency specifications is provided. Reliability and redundancy mechanisms are not detailed, though the use of RabbitMQ’s persistent message queues implies fault tolerance for data transmission. Security protocols are not explicitly described beyond the low-level driver’s safe protocol for command validation."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is deployed entirely on a **single-board computer (Raspberry Pi 4 Model B)**, with no mention of cloud, edge, or on-premise hosting platforms. Computational and storage requirements are not specified, but the system is designed for lightweight operation given its embedded deployment. Scalability mechanisms are not applicable, as the system is not described as distributed. Deployment models supported are limited to the standalone Raspberry Pi setup. Availability and reliability assurance are not detailed beyond the implied robustness of the RabbitMQ server and controller logic."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates a **safe protocol** embedded within the low-level driver to ensure the Physical Twin (PT) remains in a **safe operational state** at all times. The system employs **monitoring algorithms** to continuously assess whether the PT is performing **safely and optimally** by cross-referencing real-time sensor data with calibrated models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment. When deviations or violations are detected—such as unsafe operating conditions—the system triggers **corrective actions**, which may include human intervention or automated adjustments to maintain system integrity. The document explicitly highlights the integration of **calibration algorithms** to update models with the **best estimate of parameters**, ensuring the DT’s predictive and diagnostic capabilities remain aligned with the PT’s actual state. No explicit mention of authentication systems, threat mitigation strategies, or formal risk assessment methodologies is provided."
]
